title: The inflated figure
subtitle: ₹4.77, falling — while the quoted figure barely moves
source: RTR-WP-001, A Consolidated Real Per-Capita Series for Public Library Expenditure in India, 2014-15 to 2024-25.
caption: India's per-capita public-library expenditure, 2018-19 (the series peak) to 2024-25. The nominal figure — state MH 2205-105 spending divided by the fixed Census 2011 population, the number usually quoted — barely falls. Once RRRLF Central transfers are added, the total is deflated to constant 2011-12 rupees, and the Technical Group's actual annual population projections replace the fixed 2011 Census count, the real, consolidated figure tells a sharper story: a 42% fall from its own peak, to ₹4.77 — this campaign's headline number.

data.label: Nominal (as published)
data.note: State MH 2205-105 spending ÷ the fixed Census 2011 population — the figure usually quoted. Looks almost stable.

data.label: Real (consolidated)
data.note: State MH 2205-105 + RRRLF Central transfers, deflated to constant 2011-12 rupees, divided by the Technical Group's actual annual population projections. Falls 42% from its own peak.
